title: abutilon terminale
intro: Abutilon terminale, also known as Indian mallow or country mallow, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the Malvaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. Abutilon terminale can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, and the plant's leaves and roots are also used in traditional medicine.
